key_insight: LAMP improves Lean 4 proof synthesis by giving an agentic system explicit CoW domain knowledge at inference time, enabling kernel-verified proofs without fine-tuning.
engineering_relevance: The work shows how ontology-backed tool use and multi-agent decomposition can extend theorem provers into underrepresented mathematical domains.
method: Built an 8-module Lean 4 formalization of Combinatorics on Words with 93 core definitions and lemmas.
Used a Planner-Builder-Verifier workflow connected via MCP to a domain-specific CoW ontology.
Generated proofs with kernel checking, emphasizing structured knowledge retrieval over model retraining.
evidence_signal: On 90 CoW theorems across all 8 modules and 3 difficulty levels, LAMP verified 96.7% of proofs, with ablations costing about 12 percentage points when the tool-grounded architecture or Planner/Builder split was removed.
watch_item: Performance depends on the completeness and quality of the ontology, so coverage gaps in specialized domains could still limit proof synthesis.
paper_title: LAMP: Lean-based Agentic framework with MCP and Proof Repair
